title: Polish the dashboard TSX styling for dense session corpora
summary: Started tightening spacing, type rhythm, and density controls so the dashboard remains readable with a large session dataset instead of the small original demo.
goal: Adapt the React dashboard to the new twenty-session corpus without turning the UI into a wall of repeated card chrome.
changesMade: Reviewed long-session rendering under denser data
Outlined spacing and hierarchy adjustments for the shell and cards
Identified where repeated borders and padding feel too heavy
Mapped the CSS areas that need a more operational frontend tone

features.name: dense corpus polish
features.why: A frontend inspection tool should remain readable when the dataset grows well past the original handful of sessions.

features.name: long-content containment
features.why: Big session summaries, decisions, and file lists need stronger layout discipline in TSX.

features.name: operational visual tone
features.why: The dashboard should read like a control plane, not a generic component showcase.

decisions.decision: Polish density after the larger dataset exists
decisions.why: The real pressure points only show up once the frontend is exercised with many sessions.

decisions.decision: Keep layout changes close to the existing shell
decisions.why: This pass should refine the tool, not replace the structure that earlier sessions established.

blockers: Need screenshots or live review to validate density decisions instead of tuning purely from code.
openQuestions: Should sessions default to collapsed or expanded when the corpus is this large?
resumePrompt: Continue the density polish in dashboard/src/app/globals.css, then tune any overlong session sections in dashboard/src/components/dashboard.tsx.
